SiliconFlow vs GitHub Models

Compare SiliconFlow and GitHub Models: free credits, features, and ratings. Which one is right for you?

Feature
SiliconFlow
GitHub Models
Category
Large Language Model
Large Language Model
Rating
⭐ 4
⭐ 4
Free Credits
null RPM 500, TPM 2,000,000
150 requests/day
Refresh Period
per-minute

Watermark
No

Commercial Use
Yes
No

From ¥0.006/1K tokens

SiliconFlow

Chinese API aggregation platform unifying open-source models under one interface. Free tier: RPM 500, TPM 2M, supporting DeepSeek, Qwen, GLM and more. Saves the trouble of self-hosting open-source models, with fast domestic access.

Pros

  • ✓Accessible from China without VPN
  • ✓High concurrency: RPM 500
  • ✓Free models include GLM-Z1-9B and more
  • ✓Unified interface for easy model switching

Cons

  • ✗Limited number of free models
  • ✗Some models require payment
  • ✗Registration needed for API token

Key Features

RPM 500TPM 2MChina Direct AccessOpenAI-Compatible APIUnified Multi-Model Interface
View Details →

GitHub Models

GitHub's free AI model inference service, accessible with just a GitHub account. Supports GPT-4.1, GPT-4o and other OpenAI models for free — the lowest barrier to entry for GPT-series models.

Pros

  • ✓Free access to GPT-4.1 and GPT-4o
  • ✓Only GitHub account needed, no credit card
  • ✓Microsoft-backed stability
  • ✓Code generation and multimodal support

Cons

  • ✗Only 150 requests per day
  • ✗15 RPM rate limit
  • ✗Not suitable for high-frequency use

Key Features

Free GPT-4.1/4oGitHub Account LoginCode Model SupportPlayground Experience
View Details →
